query: Crew responding to a TCAS resolution advisory on final approach, executing a missed approach because the alert came late and ATC never called the traffic.
A resolution advisory shortly after departure that commanded a descent and triggered a ground proximity 'don't sink' alert.
Air carrier landing where a TCAS RA was immediately followed by a wind shear warning, forcing an evasive maneuver and a re-sequence.
Tower controller observing a near mid-air between two arrivals after one of them maneuvered to comply with a TCAS RA.
Air carrier approach where the crew got a terrain warning from a ground proximity system after crossing a mountain peak at a high descent rate.
B737 crew getting a controlled-flight-toward-terrain caution during a night visual approach.
Inspector reporting the station had no one qualified to do a CFM56-7B fan blade replacement, so a qualified person was sent from elsewhere.
Engine fan exit guide vane assembly on a widebody found damaged beyond repair and scrapped.
Part 107 drone operator who completed a mission in controlled airspace but failed to actually submit the LAANC request they had prepared.
Recreational drone flier who only discovered after landing that they had operated without proper LAANC authorization.
Drone pilot who flew into an active temporary flight restriction because they misunderstood their LAANC authorization.
Autopilot in lateral navigation mode capturing a false localizer signal and being slow to react, causing a track deviation on approach.
B737 approach where the autopilot made an unexpected large-angle turn while trying to intercept the course.
Flight crew finding out two days later they had carried undeclared lithium-ion cargo that went into thermal runaway downline.
Captain discovering post-flight that their copy of the dangerous-goods notification to the captain had been removed before departure.
Pilots entering the wrong navigation fix into the flight computer because two waypoints in the same area had nearly identical names.
A ramp worker refusing a supervisor's instruction to clean up a leaking hazardous-materials package, citing lack of required training.
Regional jet that failed to pressurize during climb because maintenance left a ground valve open.
Twin-turboprop losing cabin pressure in climb because the engine bleed air switches were never turned back on after a bleeds-off takeoff.
A blown main gear tire during landing rollout that damaged the underside of the wing.
A trainer who blew a nosewheel tire on landing and had to steer onto the grass because the aircraft was shimmying violently.
Crew testing an engine fluid issue on the ramp who then pushed back and departed without obtaining the final maintenance sign-off.
A light aircraft suffering total electrical failure at night, leaving the pilot reliant on a flashlight and forced to divert.
Data-entry keyboards at an air traffic facility malfunctioning after being over-sprayed during pandemic cleaning.
A flight instructor physically striking a student to make them release the controls during the takeoff roll.
Crew descending below a charted crossing-altitude restriction because the way they managed the flight guidance system for a speed request defeated the constraint.
Passengers carrying spare lithium batteries into cargo or checked bags without declaring them, slipping past gate screening.
Multiple airliners pushed back into the same ramp alley at once, blocking each other and creating tail-clearance hazards.
Military jets leaving their operating area and descending into civilian airspace without a radar handoff.
A bird ingested into an engine right at takeoff, causing heavy vibration and a return to the airport with the gear left down.
Hot air balloon blown by a sudden wind shift on landing into a tree, a roof, or other obstacle.
Pilots ending up on the wrong or a closed taxiway because the painted lines were faded and signage was poor.
Ground support vehicles failing to yield at a taxiway crossing, forcing an aircraft to brake hard to avoid a collision.
A first officer becoming incapacitated at cruise, leaving the captain to fly alone.
Cabin crew dealing with a sick passenger feared contagious but ultimately attributed to over-drinking.
A regional jet returning to land after takeoff because the landing gear would not retract.
Smoke or a burning smell coming from the control yoke on departure, prompting a return to land.
Cabin fumes making flight attendants physically ill enough to need hospital treatment after a gate return.
A rejected takeoff caused by one engine's acceleration lagging badly.
An engine failure in cruise on a light business jet leading to a single-engine diversion.
A loud bang and visible engine damage when throttles were pulled back for descent, ending in a single-engine landing.
A widebody crew getting a cargo door warning in cruise and turning back.
A maintenance technician finding a tool left behind inside the engine area during a later inspection.
A technician inadvertently discharging an engine fire-extinguisher bottle during maintenance.
An aircraft that flew in revenue service while unairworthy because ground damage was never properly evaluated.
A near mid-air collision in the training traffic pattern when another aircraft overflew and cut in.
A helicopter forced to take evasive action because another aircraft was cleared onto the same runway from the opposite end.
A helicopter that had to autorotate to a lower altitude after an ATC turn instruction put it head-on with faster traffic.
Air carrier on final spotting a lighted drone off the wing at night and reporting it to the tower.
A recreational drone operator hearing a fast-approaching helicopter and diving to land to avoid a collision.
A drone exceeding its authorized altitude while climbing near Class C airspace.
Controllers losing communication with a large drone, leaving its return-to-home plan unclear and causing a conflict.
An air ambulance helicopter having a near miss while crossing a non-towered field with a patient aboard.
A near mid-air on final at a busy airport with a helicopter lifting off from a nearby hospital pad, resulting in an RA and go-around.
A balloon that had a mid-air with a drone, which damaged the envelope and struck the pilot in the head.
A CRJ encountering severe turbulence that injured the flight crew.
A CRJ on departure trailing an A321 hitting wake turbulence strong enough to trigger the stick shaker.
A go-around prompted by encountering both wind shear and wake turbulence on approach in a turboprop.
Crew taking off with the wrong flap setting after a rushed runway change and high pre-departure workload.
An A320 losing braking on an icy ramp.
A pilot landing without a clearance.
A green laser shone into the cockpit in flight that affected someone's vision.
A possible GPS jamming event reported by a widebody crew in cruise.
An air carrier crew beginning taxi with the ground crew's headset still plugged into the aircraft.
A wingtip striking an awning support because the marshaller's parking guidance led the aircraft too close.
A baggage cart striking a parked aircraft on the ramp.
An obstacle tower near the approach end of a runway that is missing from the charts and from NOTAMs.
A solo student turning base too early after misunderstanding ATC, causing a near mid-air.
A flight attendant missing the takeoff PA and remaining unseated because of a dispute over a passenger's mask exemption.
A gear-up landing.
An air carrier altitude or speed deviation where the captain blamed reduced recent flying during the pandemic slowdown.
A small aircraft losing nav and comm at night when an alternator breaker tripped, recovered by resetting it.
